title: Drosophila Genomic DNA Isolation Using NEB Monarch Kit
author: David Loehlin

steps.title: Pre-heat a heat block or shaking...
steps.instruction: Pre-heat a heat block or shaking heat block to 56˚C before starting. Prewarm tube(s) of gDNA Elution Buffer in the heat block. For k samples, prewarm at least (k+1) * 100 µL elution buffer.

steps.title: For each sample, pipet 20 µL EDTA (0
steps.instruction: For each sample, pipet 20 µL EDTA (0.5M) into the bottom of a 1.5mL tube. Label tubes and place on ice.

steps.title: Anaesthetize flies (e
steps.instruction: Anaesthetize flies (e.g., with CO 2 ), then sweep flies into the tube. Incubate on ice . For PCR: use 1-5 flies. For Southern blot: use 10-20mg of flies.

steps.title: Prepare TLBK master mix
steps.instruction: Prepare TLBK master mix: 10 µL Proteinase K + 180 µL Tissue Lysis Buffer per sample. The enzyme is stored at -20˚C.

steps.title: For each sample:
steps.instruction: For each sample:

steps.title: Once all samples have incubated, centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Once all samples have incubated, centrifuge 3 min at maximum speed. Transfer the supernatants to new 1.5mL tubes.

steps.title: Add 3 µL RNAse A to...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL RNAse A to each lysate, vortex, then incubate 5min @ 56˚C.

steps.title: Once all samples have incubated, add...
steps.instruction: Once all samples have incubated, add 400 µL gDNA Binding Buffer to each sample. Mix thoroughly by pulse-vortexing for 5-10 seconds.

steps.title: Insert gDNA columns in collection tubes...
steps.instruction: Insert gDNA columns in collection tubes and label one per sample.

steps.title: Pipet each binding-buffer-mixed sample onto the...
steps.instruction: Pipet each binding-buffer-mixed sample onto the column matrix, without touching the upper column area.

steps.title: Cap and centrifuge twice
steps.instruction: Cap and centrifuge twice: first for 3 minutes at 1,000 g, then for 1 minute at max.

steps.title: Transfer the column to a clean collection tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the column to a clean collection tube. If the column touches the flow-through, re-spin. Pour the flow-through into liquid waste and discard the old collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL gDNA Wash Buffer to each column
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL gDNA Wash Buffer to each column. Cap and invert so that the wash buffer reaches the cap.

steps.title: Centrifuge 1 min at max
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 1 min at max.

steps.title: Pipet the flow-through into liquid waste...
steps.instruction: Pipet the flow-through into liquid waste then reinsert the column in the collection tube. Can re-use a dirty tip for this, or a vacuum aspirator.

steps.title: Add 500 µL gDNA Wash Buffer then cap
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL gDNA Wash Buffer then cap. Centrifuge 2 min at max.

steps.title: Transfer each column to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer each column to a clean 1.5mL tube. Do not bend the tube caps!

steps.title: Using a clean filter tip for...
steps.instruction: Using a clean filter tip for each sample, add 100 µL of warm Elution Buffer directly to the column matrix. [for Southern: use 35 µL]. Incubate at room temperature for 1+ minute.

steps.title: Load the centrifuge with the 1
steps.instruction: Load the centrifuge with the 1.5mL tubes + columns so that the tube caps trail the direction of rotation. Cap the rotor lid and centrifuge for 1 min at max. The DNA is now in the tube, so discard the column. Cap tubes, label them, and store gDNA at -20˚C.

steps.title: Grab a clean microtube pestle and grind the flies
steps.instruction: Grab a clean microtube pestle and grind the flies. For best results, crush the flies between the conical part of the pestle and the conical sidewall of the tube – keep them off the bottom. The goal is to smush all the soft internal organs and get the cells into suspension. Grind until all major bug parts are fragmented (5-40 twists). If a fly gets stuck at the bottom of the tube, the pestle can be used as a piston to pull up the liquid there. Or use a pipet tip.

steps.title: Add 190 µL of TLBK to...
steps.instruction: Add 190 µL of TLBK to the ground tissue, then vortex immediately and thoroughly so that nothing is stuck to the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Incubate sample in 56˚C heat block...
steps.instruction: Incubate sample in 56˚C heat block for 30 minutes to 3 hours, vortexing every 5-15min (or in shaking heat block at ~1400 RPM). Proceed to grind the next sample.
